description: Single configuration record describing how an external frontend integrates with this Frappe backend over HTTP. This DocType documents and surfaces the integration; the real linking uses native Frappe tools (User API Access, OAuth Client, Webhook).

fields.description: Master switch indicating that an external frontend is integrated with this backend. This is a documentation/coordination flag; it does not start or stop the API.
fields.label: Integration Enabled

fields.description: Public base URL of the external frontend that consumes this backend, for example https://salis-fleet.com.
fields.label: Frontend Base URL

fields.description: Version of the documented API contract the frontend is built against. Additive changes are safe; breaking changes increment this value and require a notice to integrators.
fields.label: API Contract Version

fields.description: CORS origin(s) an administrator should add to allow_cors in site_config.json so the frontend can call this backend, for example https://salis-fleet.com. This field only records the intended origins; it does not modify server configuration.
fields.label: Allowed Origins (for site_config allow_cors)

fields.default:
Integrating an external frontend

This app is the backend and source of truth. An external frontend (any stack: React, Vue, Flutter, native mobile, etc.) consumes it over HTTP. The full developer starter kit lives in docs/INTEGRATION.md in the application repository.

The real linking uses native Frappe tools — do not reimplement them here:

  • Token auth: open a User record, generate an API Key + API Secret under API Access, and send the header Authorization: token <api_key>:<api_secret>. Token auth is stateless and CSRF-exempt, so it works reliably from an external origin.
  • OAuth Client: use the native OAuth Client DocType if you prefer an OAuth 2.0 authorization-code flow instead of API keys.
  • Webhook: use the native Webhook DocType to push outbound events to the frontend (or any service) when documents change.

Remember to add the frontend origin to allow_cors in site_config.json.
fields.label: Integration Help

name: Apex Integration Settings
